summary: Added JetBrains IDE metadata ignore patterns to the repository .gitignore, based on GitHub's Global/JetBrains.gitignore, to prevent user-specific PyCharm, CLion, Rider, IntelliJ, and related IDE files from entering the decomp repository.
what_changed: Appended a JetBrains IDE ignore block to .gitignore.
Ignored user-specific .idea files such as workspace.xml, tasks.xml, usage.statistics.xml, dictionaries, shelf, aws.xml, and contentModel.xml.
Ignored sensitive or high-churn JetBrains metadata such as dataSources, sqlDataSources.xml, dynamic.xml, uiDesigner.xml, and dbnavigator.xml.
Added ignore patterns for IDE-generated build and project artifacts including cmake-build-*/, *.iws, out/, .idea_modules/, .idea/httpRequests, and .idea/caches/build_file_checksums.ser.
Included commented-out optional ignore rules for Gradle or Maven auto-import module files, preserving upstream guidance rather than enabling them by default.
smart_moves: Used the established GitHub global JetBrains.gitignore as the source, reducing the risk of an incomplete or project-specific ad hoc ignore list.
Kept optional Gradle/Maven module exclusions commented out, avoiding over-broad ignores for project files that might be intentionally versioned in some workflows.
Grouped ignore entries with explanatory comments, making future maintenance and audits easier.
Covered multiple JetBrains IDEs relevant to decomp contributors, especially CLion and PyCharm, without requiring each contributor to maintain local excludes.
decomp_lessons: Repository hygiene matters in decomp projects because contributors often use different local IDEs and generated metadata can create noisy diffs unrelated to matching work.
Prefer adding common IDE-generated files to .gitignore when they are clearly user-specific, sensitive, or high-churn.
When importing a standard ignore template, retain source comments or links so future contributors know whether entries came from an upstream-maintained convention.
Do not ignore potentially meaningful project files blindly; leave conditional patterns commented when the template indicates they depend on project setup.
naming_conventions: JetBrains project metadata commonly lives under .idea/.
JetBrains CMake build directories commonly match cmake-build-*/.
IntelliJ-style output directories may use out/.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal review feedback were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modified .gitignore after the JetBrains metadata block.
Search for committed .idea files or cmake-build directories before PR #431.
Check whether later tooling documentation recommends global excludes versus repository-level IDE ignores.
